_meta.description: All 2026 tax rates, brackets, and thresholds used by the deterministic tax calculator. No AI needed — pure math inputs.

gst_qst_notes: Threshold is $30,000 in taxable worldwide supplies over any 4 consecutive calendar quarters
Once threshold is crossed, registration is mandatory within 30 days
Some supplies are zero-rated (basic groceries) or exempt — home bakers selling at markets must verify with Revenu Québec

qpp.note: Self-employed pay BOTH the employee (6.4%) and employer (6.4%) shares = 12.8% total. This surprises most first-time self-employed people.
qpp.plain_language: If you earn $50,000 self-employed, you pay QPP on ($50,000 - $3,500) = $46,500 × 12.8% = $5,952. An employee earning the same would only pay $2,976.

qpip.note: Quebec Parental Insurance Plan — provides maternity/paternity benefits. Self-employed pay a single combined rate.
qpip.benefit: Unlike CPP, QPIP actually provides parental leave benefits to self-employed workers who pay in

federal_self_employed_deduction: Half of QPP contributions are deductible on federal return (line 22200)
quebec_abatement_note: Quebec residents receive a 16.5% federal tax abatement because Quebec funds its own programs (QPP, QPIP, etc.) instead of federal ones

corporate_tax_rate_small_business.note: Combined ~12.2% on first $500K of active business income for a Quebec CCPC (Canadian-Controlled Private Corporation)

quarterly_installment_note: If you expect to owe more than $3,000 in federal tax (or $1,800 in Quebec), you must make quarterly installments. Due: March 15, June 15, September 15, December 15.
